title: Discrete-Time Hazard Instead Of Only Binary Next-Lap
summary: Discrete-time survival models are designed for integer event times and censoring, which matches lap-by-lap pit timing better than a single binary label.
rationale: Discrete-time survival models are designed for integer event times and censoring, which matches lap-by-lap pit timing better than a single binary label. This is a distinct survival framing from the current AFT approach and may better model the opening and closing of pit windows.
implementation_hint: Expand each driver-race-stint into person-period rows for the remaining laps, or approximate with multi-horizon heads such as `pit_in_1`, `pit_in_2`, `pit_in_3`. Train a logistic/cloglog hazard model and recover `P(PitNextLap)` as the first-step hazard; feed later-step hazards into the stacker too.
expected_effect: Sharper probabilities around pit-window boundaries and improved calibration for cars that are clearly approaching, but not yet at, the stop lap.
risk: Row explosion can slow training substantially, and fold construction must be leak-safe at the stint/race level.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
